title: Added check for se is not None, otherwise we get an error if running without stellar evolution.
description:
Simple hotfix adding an if statement to check if the stellar evolution object se is not None when initializing the state.stars\_to\_se and state.se\_to\_stars channel. This fixes a crash that happens if running without stellar evolution. Also added code that initialized the cross section of FUV/EUV photons to non-zero values preventing a convergence issue occurring in VETTAM if the user chooses to run radiation without turning stellar evolution off.

Checklist for merging:

* :white_check_mark: code successfully builds and run on Snellius, and ideally >=1 other cluster too
* :white_check_mark: you have announced your changes at a torch user's group meeting
* :white_check_mark: at least 1 other person reviewed and approved your PR

‌
author: Brooke Polak

comments.content: With the new if statement, the code still runs successfully if se is not None. Note however that if se is None, the radiation solver is still called despite the FUV/ionizing fluxes being set to 0. This prevents VETTAM from converging. It is not an issue before we merge the vettam branch into main, but I would suggest that we also use those new wrappers to ensure feedback is not called without SE, or to ensure in another way that this is compatible with VETTAM.

We still need radiation and winds to be injected in the test cases where we set a stars feedback properties by hand and don’t want them altered by se. I will try to reproduce this behavior on my end and figure out another solution.

Ok, I think I found a good solution. The radiation and wind properties are initialized in torch\_se.py, while other seba properties are initialized in torch\_sf.py. If se=none, the rad/wind properties aren’t set, so they are garbage uninitialized floats giving vettam issues. So I will set the rad/wind properties to zero in torch\_sf.py, which can be reset in torch\_se if se==none.

Now that I think about this again, I’m not sure there is a problem… if se==none and RadTrans=T, then the user should be supplying stars with valid values of nion/eion. If the user wants stars with no radiation, in the case of se==none and no provided eion/nion/etc values, then the user should simply turn radiation off entirely because their use case is without source particles. Right, or am I missing something?

@{5ce728028ad0f60dca00fd3a} Conceptually, you’re right. I think the question is whether we want to force a crash \(with an error message\) if those values aren’t supplied, or just let it try to do feedback while the values aren’t set. I think the safest thing is to set the rad/wind properties to 0 in torch\_sf, as you suggested above. For test cases, we would likely be using `restart_with_user_ICs`, where we can manually set nion/eion/dmdt if needed. 

‌

‌
The code now runs properly and can complete several timesteps after star formation, with stellar evolution on and off.

There was a secondary re-defining of sigDust is torch\_se.py, that set the particle property SPEP\_PART\_PROP. In fervent, sigDust from the flash.par file is what is actually used. This PR sets the particle property of SPEP to the user defined value in flash.par.

SPEP\_PART\_PROP isn’t actually used anywhere in the code. It gets passed around a few times in FERVENT, but as FERVENT is now deprecated, this isn’t worth addressing. title: Condensed fervent raytrace toggle into one user parameter, deprecated ray folder that excludes FUV band
description:
This PR addresses Issues [#27](https://bitbucket.org/torch-sf/torch/issues/27/remove-rt_raytrace-xor-useradtransfer) and [#70](https://bitbucket.org/torch-sf/torch/issues/70/units-are-wrong-in-comment-for-fufl_var).

**ISSUE** [**#27**](https://bitbucket.org/torch-sf/torch/issues/27/remove-rt_raytrace-xor-useradtransfer)**:**

There are two user parameters with FERVENT that do the same thing, they toggle raytracing. They are useRadTransfer and rt\_rayTrace. Because FERVENT is deprecated, its not worth the effort to remove one entirely. Rather, I simply load rt\_rayTrace into the useRadTransfer variable. rt\_rayTrace is used in the heating and cooling routine and VETTAM, which is why I chose to keep it.

**ISSUE # 70:**

A comment had incorrect units for flux variables which is now fixed.

**OTHER:**

I deprecated the ray folder. We only use ray\_pe, which includes the FUV band, and keeping the other folder around just leads to confusion and user error.
